title: Phase 1 Foundation Layer (Tier 1 - High Density) — Buddhist Consciousness Architecture

concepts.title: Buddhist Consciousness Architecture: Five Skandhas + 18 Ayatanas + Mano-Dhatu vs Mano-Vijnana
concepts.description: The complete structure of how consciousness assembles and perpetuates itself moment by moment through five bundled patterns and eighteen sensory-mental gateways

concepts.title: Elemental Consciousness Principles: Five Elements as Consciousness Operators
concepts.description: Earth, Water, Fire, Air, Space as five distinct operating modes of consciousness, not material substances; maps to Five Skandhas and Five Klesa

concepts.title: Mandala Architecture: The Four Forms as Consciousness Blueprints
concepts.description: The Maha, Samaya, Karma, and Dharma mandalas as four levels of encoding consciousness-structure into visual-sonic-kinetic forms

concepts.title: Vajramukti as Buddhist Martial Philosophy: Fighting as Consciousness-Work
concepts.description: Buddhist martial art as accelerated consciousness-transformation through direct physical confrontation

concepts.title: Prasada: Buddhist Consciousness-Based Energy, Distinct from Prana and Chi
concepts.description: Energy that arises only where consciousness is clear; fundamental distinction from Hindu prana and Taoist chi

concepts.title: Four Noble Truths
concepts.description: Suffering, its cause, cessation, and the path

concepts.title: Chaitanya-Atma — Consciousness-Self Equation
concepts.description: Vasugupta's opening: consciousness IS self, not metaphorically; immediate verifiable fact, not aspiration

concepts.title: Panchashakti — The Five Powers of Consciousness
concepts.description: Cit, Ānanda, Icchā, Jñāna, Kriyā as the five powers operative in every conscious being; all beings divine by virtue of possessing will, knowledge, action

concepts.title: Icchā Śakti — Desire as the Subsuming Power
concepts.description: Desire is the primary power; Jñāna and Kriyā flow from Icchā; longing as the deepest motivation; fullness expressing itself

concepts.title: Jñāna vs Kriyā Reconciliation
concepts.description: Interior and exterior practices as equal expressions of Icchā; both paths valid; temperament determines natural emphasis

concepts.title: Three Upāyas — Śāmbhava, Śakta, Āṇava
concepts.description: Bliss-meditation, energy-work, disciplined effort as three legitimate modes; permission structure for practitioner self-selection; all three expressions of Icchā

concepts.title: Anugraha and Nigraha — Divine Grace and Concealment
concepts.description: God's dual functions: revealing and hiding your nature; both necessary; both deepen longing and recognition

concepts.title: God as Desiring Consciousness
concepts.description: God is desire-expressing; creation from fullness not need; divine responsiveness to sincere longing

concepts.title: Maya — Divine Play and Expression
concepts.description: Creation as God's play from abundance; all experience (joy and suffering) as divine expression; universe as God knowing herself through manifested forms

concepts.title: Aesthetic Cultivation as Spiritual Practice
concepts.description: Beauty as non-optional foundation; aesthetic refinement as preparation for inner pūjā; sensory dimensions as entry point to subtle perception

concepts.title: Nidhāsana — Meditation on the Formless
concepts.description: Jñāna transmission: resting consciousness in formless, concept-free reality; not visualization or mantra; direct recognition beyond all forms

concepts.title: SalaVrka — Temple Wolves, Guardians of Sacred Enclosure
concepts.description: Plural, masculine, stationed guardians; Indra's wolf transformation (TS VI 2 4); howling as operative expulsion; operative now, not mythic-only

concepts.title: Sarama — The Celestial She-Wolf, Divine Scout and Refuser
concepts.description: Independent reconnaissance operator; refusal of seduction through iron will and clarity; RV X 8; sons become Yama's hounds

concepts.title: RudraGanika — The Howling Women, Female Retinue of Rudra
concepts.description: Feminine collective parallel to SalaVrka; comradely equals, not hierarchical; AV-S XII 5 (funeral context), AV-S XI 2 (wolf-hymnal co-evocation); identical operative function (Ailaba unification)

concepts.title: Bhairava and Bhairava Sadhana
concepts.description: Added "Vedic Wolf Protection Doctrine" section mapping protective lineage (Sarama → her sons → Rudra Vastopati → Bhairava/Kshetrapala) and unified system (+600 words)

concepts.title: Bhairava Kshetrapala — Guardian of Sacred Space
concepts.description: Expanded "Canine Entourage" section from 220 words to 900+ words with full Vedic material, system structure, and SalaVrka/RudraGanika/Sarama integration

concepts.title: Icchā Śakti — Desire as the Subsuming Power
concepts.description: Divine will as the primary power underlying both knowing and doing

concepts.title: Jñāna vs Kriyā Reconciliation
concepts.description: How the knowledge path and ritual path express the same underlying power; resolution of the interior/exterior dichotomy
